text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a software development practice where developers regularly merge their code changes into a shared repository. Each merge triggers an automated build and a series of tests. The main goal is to catch issues early, before they become bigger problems. A CI pipeline coordinates these steps, starting with the build, then running unit and integration tests. If a test fails, the pipeline stops, and the team is immediately notified. This helps developers fix bugs quickly, ensuring the software remains stable. By automating these checks, teams spend less time troubleshooting and more time building new features.
